description:
You are assisting a behavioral-science researcher who just downloaded several tab-separated value (TSV) datasets and now needs to store them in a structured project workspace.

  STARTING POINT
  • Three TSV files are already present in /home/user/downloads  
      – demographics.tsv  
      – reaction_times.tsv  
      – survey_responses.tsv  
  • No other .tsv files are in /home/user/downloads.  
  • The directory /home/user/projects/ does exist, but /home/user/projects/behavioral_study/ does not.

  WHAT YOU MUST DO  
  1. Create the directory tree exactly as shown (all names are case-sensitive):  
       /home/user/projects/behavioral_study/  
           ├── datasets  
           ├── scripts  
           └── docs

  2. Move (not copy) every *.tsv file currently located in /home/user/downloads/ into  
       /home/user/projects/behavioral_study/datasets/  

     After this step there must be **zero** .tsv files left inside /home/user/downloads.

  3. Inside /home/user/projects/behavioral_study/ create a plain-text file named dataset_manifest.txt whose exact contents follow the template below.  
     • List ONLY the filenames of the TSV files (no paths), one per line, sorted in ascending alphabetical order.  
     • After the final filename line, add a last line of the form  
           FILE COUNT: N  
       where N is the integer number of TSV files you moved.  
     • Do not include blank lines or extra spaces.  
     • Example format (your filenames and count will differ):  
           alpha.tsv  
           bravo.tsv  
           charlie.tsv  
           FILE COUNT: 3

  4. Double-check that /home/user/projects/behavioral_study/datasets/ now contains the TSV files and that dataset_manifest.txt exactly matches the required format.

  The automated tests will verify:  
  • The directory structure exists and is named exactly as specified.  
  • All three TSV files have been moved (not copied) to the datasets directory.  
  • /home/user/downloads/ contains no *.tsv files.  
  • /home/user/projects/behavioral_study/dataset_manifest.txt exists and its contents are an alphabetically sorted list of the filenames followed by the correctly calculated FILE COUNT line, with no extra whitespace or blank lines.

  Remember: do NOT display your shell commands in the final answer; just execute them in the terminal.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files before the agent runs -->
  <initial_files>
    <file path="/home/user/downloads/demographics.tsv">
      id\tage\tgender\n1\t34\tF\n2\t29\tM\n
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/downloads/reaction_times.tsv">
      participant_id\ttime_ms\n1\t532\n2\t489\n
    </file>
    <file path="/home/user/downloads/survey_responses.tsv">
      pid\tq1\tq2\tq3\n1\t4\t3\t5\n2\t5\t4\t4\n
    </file>
  </initial_files>

  <!-- Expected final state -->
  <final_state>
    <!-- Directory tree -->
    <dir path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/"/>
    <dir path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/datasets"/>
    <dir path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/scripts"/>
    <dir path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/docs"/>

    <!-- TSV files must be inside datasets -->
    <file path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/datasets/demographics.tsv" source="/home/user/downloads/demographics.tsv"/>
    <file path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/datasets/reaction_times.tsv" source="/home/user/downloads/reaction_times.tsv"/>
    <file path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/datasets/survey_responses.tsv" source="/home/user/downloads/survey_responses.tsv"/>

    <!-- downloads directory now lacks TSVs -->
    <empty dir="/home/user/downloads" pattern="*.tsv"/>

    <!-- Manifest file -->
    <file path="/home/user/projects/behavioral_study/dataset_manifest.txt">
demographics.tsv
reaction_times.tsv
survey_responses.tsv
FILE COUNT: 3
    </file>
  </final_state>
